title: Blend specialists with regime-gated rank averaging, not generic calibration
summary: Official scikit-learn docs note calibration generally should not improve ROC AUC, and isotonic can even change ranks via ties.
rationale: Official scikit-learn docs note calibration generally should not improve ROC AUC, and isotonic can even change ranks via ties. For this metric, the better use of extra modeling complexity is regime-gated blending of orthogonal experts, not post-hoc calibration for its own sake.
implementation_hint: Train a small set of decorrelated experts: baseline binary GBM, competing-risks hazard, and regime/cascade expert. Generate strictly out-of-fold predictions by race groups, then learn simple gate weights by inferred regime and race phase. Use rank averaging or sigmoid/logit stacking; avoid isotonic unless it proves LB-positive.
expected_effect: Improves robustness by letting rare-regime experts contribute only when relevant while preserving the strong baseline ordering on ordinary green-flag dry laps.
risk: Too many experts or flexible gates will overfit the CV split. Keep the gate low-capacity, require fold-pure meta-features, and verify that any blend beats the best single model on grouped CV.
